channel.title: geocaching on ~ The Tobyblog 📝
channel.description: Recent content in geocaching on ~ The Tobyblog 📝

item.title: Where O’Conner Ends
item.description: The geocache hidden in this location is no longer listed. I wonder why. Yet there it is, in perfect condition. Double-boxed, dry, with a full log and compliment of toys.

item.title: Time To Start It Up Again
item.description: I’ve been too long out of the game.

item.title: Geocaching Lost Episodes
item.description: Over the years I’ve accumulated a ton of never-used footage from our various geocaching adventures. Here they all are, wrapped up a big ball of video for you, my Tobyblogging public. Enjoy.

item.title: My Geocoin Collection
item.description: You might find this interesting if you’re a hardcore geocaching or geocoin enthusiast, or an ASMR type.

item.title: The Tobyblog Thanksgiving Special
item.description: After taking some time off to contemplate the overwhelming success of my Burger King poop tweet*, we now return to our regularly scheduled programming. In truth October is always a busy month for us and I don’t have much spare time for blogging. This past Halloween was without a doubt the best yet, but I’ll save that retrospective for another day. For today we find ourselves at the end of November, as grand a month as any.

item.title: Super Mega Fun Pack
item.description: The Super Mega Fun Pack geocache (GC5Z4X7) is a thinly-veiled tribute to Matt of dinosaurdracula.com and the amazing box of goodies that he puts together every month for his hordes of devoted fans. It’s only “veiled” at all because of the (understandable) policy of geocaching.com not to list any cache with references to commercial products. After going back and forth with them a bit about this, I relented and removed any overt references to Matt or his website, including changing the name of the cache from the original “The Dino Drac Fun Pack Geocache” to “Super Mega Fun Pack”.

item.title: March Cache Maintenance
item.description: TheVCubeSolver and I hit the road on a pretty March morning to do some maintenance on a couple of our geocaches. The log for RtW #3 had filled up and Augie’s beaver cache had recently been reported missing. A little town, a little country. More geocaching goodness for you.
